english: _gró-ba ('gro ba), to walk behind or after another person, Pth. -- pýi (phyi) _bráṅ ('brang) Lex. (also mčis-bráṅ (mchis brang)), spouse, wife. -- pýi-ma (phyi ma), the posterior Schr.(?) -- pýi-bźin (phyi bzhin) adv. and postp., after; _gró-ba ('gro ba), _bráṅ-ba ('brang ba), frq.; rí-dags-kyi (ri dags kyi) pýi-bźin (phyi bzhin) rgyúg-pa (rgyug pa), to pursue game, deer; pýir-bźin (phyir bzhin), id.; pýi-la (phyi la), later lit. and C., id.; ... kyi (kyi) pýi-bźin (phyi bzhin) pýin-pa (phyin pa), _óṅ-ba ('ong ba), _gró-ba ('gro ba), to go after; v. also pýir (phyir) and pýis (phyis). II. after; adv.: sṅa-pýi (snga phyi), sooner and later; also adj.: the former, the latter; the earlier, the later; _di-pýi ('di phyi) sc. tse (tshe), the present and the future life; frq.; dus (dus) pýi (phyi) źig-na (zhig na), at a later period, some time afterwards Dzl.; deï (de'i) pýi (phyi) nyin (nyin) on the following day Dzl.; nyí-ma (nyi ma) deï (de'i) pýi (phyi) de (de) nyín (nyin) kó-na (kho na), id., Tar. -- pýi-dgra (phyi dgra) v. dgra (dgra). -- pýi-čad (phyi chad) = pýin-čad (phyin chad) q.v. -- pýi-tog (phyi tog) W., the later part of the afternoon. -- pýi-dro (phyi dro), pýi-ro (phyi ro) (also Mil.) W., gen. *pí-tog (pi tog), pí-ro (pi ro)* id., also evening. -- pýi-nas (phyi nas), in future, in time to come, Mil. -- pýi-před (phyi phred) Tar.: nyi-ma (nyi ma) pýi-před-kyi (phyi phred kyi) bar-du (bar du) Schf., until sunset; Schr.: evening. -- pýi-pýág (phyi phyag) byéd-pa (byed pa), to greet for the last time, to bid farewell, to take leave. -- pýi-ma (phyi ma) adj.: later, subsequent, following, sṅá-ma (snga ma) ma (ma) śu (zhu) pýi-ma (phyi ma) zá-ba (za ba), not having digested the first (meal), to eat (consecutive) additional quantities Lt.; pýi-ma (phyi ma) pýi-ma (phyi ma), each following one, every one consecutive in a series, S.g. and elsewh.; nyál-bai (nyal ba'i) pýi-ma (phyi ma), the last going to bed, Mil.; pýi-ma-rnams (phyi ma rnams), the later ones, the moderns, frq. -- pýi-mo (phyi mo) adj. late, da (da) (nyi-ma (nyi ma)) pýi-mór (phyi mor) soṅ (song) dúg-pas (dug pas), it having grown late (in the day) Mil.; *'i go ('i go) pí-mo (pi mo) p'e dug (phe dug)*, this door is not opened until later (in the day), W. *pí-mo (pi mo) čó(s)-kaṅ-ni (cho(s) kang ni) tá-gir (ta gir)*, the last baked, newest bread, W. -- pýi-rabs (phyi rabs), the later generation, posterity. Cf. pýin (phyin), pýis (phyis). III. outside, pýiï (phyi'i) źiṅ (zhing), the field outside, as a third part of the property, exclusive of cattle and money (cf. sgo (sgo) init.); pýiï (phyi'i) só-nam (so nam), husbandry, farming Glr.; pýiï-rgya-mtso (phyi'i rgya mtsho), the outer sea, the ocean, Glr.; pýiï (phyi'i) mi (mi) Dzl. (Ms.), people from abroad, other, strange people, not belonging to the family, mgrón-nam (mgron nam) pýi-mi-dag (phyi mi dag) _óṅs-na ('ongs na), if (when) guests or strangers come, Dzl.; pýi-na (phyi na), out of doors, abroad; pýi-nas (phyi nas), from without, from abroad; pýi-ru (phyi ru), pýir (phyir), out (proceeding from the interior of a place to the exterior), less frq., v. pýi-rol (phyi rol); pýi-la (phyi la), id., B. and C. frq. pýi-kyóg (phyi kyog) Sch.: with knees bent outward. -- pýi-gliṅ (phyi gling) v. rgya-pi-liṅ (rgya pi ling) under rgya (rgya) comp.; pýi-dgrá (phyi dgra) v. dgra (dgra). -- *pí-(s)ta-la (pi (s)ta la)* and *-ru (-ru)*. W. for pýi-ról-na (phyi rol na) etc.; *pí-stu-la (pi stu la) čá-če (cha che)*, euphemist. for 'going to the watercloset'. -- pýi-náṅ (phyi nang), the outside and inside *pí naṅ lóg-če* (pi nang log che), W., bsgyúr-ba (bsgyur ba), Schr. to turn inside out, e.g. a bag; lčágs-kyi (lcags kyi) sgróm (sgrom) la-sógs-pa (la sogs pa) sgrom (sgrom) pýi (phyi) naṅ (nang) rim-pa (rim pa) bdun (bdun) tsam (tsam) an iron box (coffin) and moreover a series of 7 boxes one within the other Tar. 28 pýi (phyi) naṅ (nang) ynyis-ka (gnyis ka) smín-pa (smin pa), ripe both as to the outside and inside, Dzl.; pýi (phyi) naṅ (nang) ytsaṅ (gtsang), pure as to thought and action. With respect to religion, this expression generally denotes the difference between Non-Buddhism -- or in a more limited sense Brahmanism -- and Buddhism; frequently ysaṅ (gsang) is added as a third item, being explained by: pýi (phyi) lus (lus) naṅ (nang) ṅag (ngag) ysáṅ-ba (gsang ba) yid (yid), which explanation however is insufficient, e.g. in the passage: čos (chos) pýi (phyi) naṅ (nang) ysaṅ (gsang) Pth., in which moreover merely a classification within the Buddhist religion seems to be spoken of. Political distinctions are made in Glr.: pýi (phyi) naṅ (nang) bar (bar) ysúm-gyi (gsum gyi) byá-ba (bya ba) byéd-paï (byed pa'i) blón-po (blon po), yet without sufficiently elucidating the subject. The terms pýi (phyi) lta (lta) and naṅ (nang) lta (lta), Glr. fol. 89, as well as pýi (phyi) ltár-du (ltar du) and naṅ (nang) ltár-du (ltar du), Pth. p. 10 I am at a loss to explain. -- pýi-pa (phyi pa) 1. B. and col. a Non-Buddhist, more particularly a Brahmanist, also for pýi-paï (phyi pa'i) čos (chos), the doctrine of Brahma pýi-pa-la (phyi pa la) dga (dga') Glr. 2. Chr. Prot.: heathen, one that is neither a jew nor a Christian. -- pýi-yul (phyi yul) 1. Sch. foreign country. 2. pýi (phyi) snáṅ-bai (snang ba'i) yul (yul), the external world, opp. to: náṅ-gi (nang gi) sems (sems), Mil. -- pýi-rol (phyi rol), 1. the outside, mál-gyi (mal gyi) pýi-rol (phyi rol), the outside of the bed, Glr.; pýi-rol-na (phyi rol na), -tu (-tu), -nas (-nas), in B. gen. for pýi-na (phyi na), -ru (-ru), -nas (-nas); adv. outside, out of doors, out, from without; postp. on the outside before (the door), (he was turned) out
